# cmd0 - Complete Documentation

> A desktop AI agent that lives in your menubar. Summon it with a keystroke to search the web, run commands, automate browsers, and extend with pi extensions.

## Overview

cmd0 is an open-source desktop AI agent built with the [pi-coding-agent](https://github.com/mariozechner/pi-coding-agent) framework. It provides a floating chat interface that can be summoned from anywhere using a global hotkey, giving you instant access to AI-powered tools for web search, browser automation, terminal commands, and more.

## Key Features

### Core Capabilities
- **Web Search**: DuckDuckGo integration for instant research
- **Browser Automation**: Playwright-powered Chrome control
- **System Access**: Execute bash commands, read/write files
- **Desktop Notifications**: Send native OS notifications
- **Screenshots**: Capture and attach screen regions
- **Self-Modification**: Edit source code via `/0` command
- **Task Scheduling**: Background daemon for recurring tasks
- **State Management**: Snapshots and restore functionality
- **Safe Mode**: `--safe` flag for restricted operation

### Extensions
- **pi-notetaker**: Meeting transcription and AI summaries using local Whisper

## Installation

### Prerequisites
- macOS or Linux
- Node.js 18+
- npm or yarn
- Git

### Quick Install

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/dvrosalesm/cmd0.git
cd cmd0

# Run the installer
./install.sh
```

The installer will:
1. Install npm dependencies
2. Compile TypeScript
3. Register global hotkey
4. Launch the agent

### Manual Installation

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Build the project
npm run build

# Or run in development mode
npm run dev
```

## Configuration

### API Keys

cmd0 requires an API key from either OpenRouter or Fireworks AI:

**OpenRouter** (keys start with `sk-or-...`)
- Default model: `minimax/minimax-m2.7`
- Supports Claude, GPT-4, Llama, and more

**Fireworks AI** (keys start with `fw_...`)
- Default model: `accounts/fireworks/routers/kimi-k2p5-turbo`
- Fast inference with Kimi and Llama models

On first launch, cmd0 will prompt for your API key. Configuration is stored at `~/.cmd0/data/config.json`.

### Environment Variables

```bash
# Optional: Pre-configure API key
export OPENROUTER_API_KEY=sk-or-...
export FIREWORKS_API_KEY=fw_...
```

## Usage

### Basic Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `Cmd+0` / `Super+0` | Summon the agent |
| `/0 <instruction>` | Self-modification mode |
| `/cancel` | Stop current operation |
| `/tasks` | Manage background tasks |
| `/snap <name>` | Save state snapshot |
| `/restore <name>` | Restore from snapshot |
| `/safe` | Enter safe mode |

### Example Interactions

```
You: Search for Python async best practices
cmd0: [Performs DuckDuckGo search and summarizes results]

You: /0 add a dark theme toggle
 cmd0: [Reads source, adds theme code, reloads]

You: Take a screenshot of the terminal
 cmd0: [Captures screen region, attaches to chat]
```

## Architecture

### Project Structure

```
cmd0/
├── src/
│   ├── main.ts           # Electron main process
│   ├── renderer.ts       # UI logic
│   ├── style.css         # Styling
│   └── features/         # Tool modules
│       ├── anima.ts      # Self-modification
│       ├── web.ts        # Web search/fetch
│       ├── browser.ts    # Playwright automation
│       ├── system.ts     # Notifications/screenshots
│       └── tasks.ts      # Background tasks
├── docs/                 # Documentation
│   ├── index.html        # Landing page
│   ├── llms.txt         # LLM overview
│   └── llms-full.txt    # This file
└── package.json
```

### Self-Modification System

The `anima` feature enables cmd0 to modify its own source code:

1. Source files live in `~/.cmd0/anima/`
2. User issues `/0` command with instructions
3. LLM reads relevant files via `anima_read`
4. LLM proposes changes via `anima_write`
5. `anima_reload` syncs, recompiles, and restarts

This creates a feedback loop where the agent can evolve based on user needs.

### Data Directory

```
~/.cmd0/
├── anima/              # Live source code
├── data/
│   ├── config.json    # Settings and API keys
│   └── meetings/      # pi-notetaker recordings
├── snapshots/         # Saved states
└── tasks.md          # Background tasks
```

## pi-notetaker Extension

### Overview

pi-notetaker is a pi extension for meeting transcription and AI-powered summaries. It records audio locally, transcribes using Whisper, and generates structured summaries using the active LLM.

### Installation

```bash
# Copy extension to pi extensions directory
cp -r pi-notetaker ~/.pi/agent/extensions/

# Or for cmd0, place in project and reference
```

### Setup

```
/meeting setup
```

This installs:
- sox (audio recording)
- whisper-cpp (transcription)
- Downloads base.en model (~142MB)

### Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/meeting start [name]` | Begin recording |
| `/meeting stop` | Stop and process |
| `/meeting list` | View past meetings |
| `/meeting view <id>` | Read summary |
| `/meeting transcript <id>` | Raw transcript |

### Privacy

All processing is local:
- Audio recorded to local file
- Whisper runs on-device
- No cloud transcription services
- No data leaves your machine

## Security & Safety

### Safe Mode

Launch with `--safe` to:
- Disable self-modification
- Restore base source files
- Prevent accidental changes

### Snapshots

Before major changes:
```
/snap before-experiment
# ... make changes ...
/restore before-experiment  # If needed
```

### Best Practices

1. Create snapshots before `/0` modifications
2. Review proposed code changes
3. Test in safe mode if unsure
4. Keep backups of working states

## Development

### Building

```bash
# TypeScript compilation
npx tsc

# Or with watch mode
npx tsc --watch
```

### Adding Features

1. Create feature in `src/features/my-feature.ts`
2. Export a `Feature` object with `name`, `description`, `createTools`
3. Import and add to `ALL_FEATURES` in `src/features/index.ts`
4. Test and reload

### Debugging

```bash
# Enable Electron debugging
npm run dev -- --inspect

# View logs
tail -f ~/.cmd0/logs/app.log
```

## Troubleshooting

### Hotkey Not Working (Linux)

On Wayland/Hyprland, use the window rule:
```
bind = SUPER, 0, exec, cmd0
```

### Permission Errors

```bash
# Fix permissions
chmod +x ./install.sh
chmod +x ./cmd0-launcher.sh
```

### Rebuild After Modification Failures

```bash
# If anima_reload fails
cd ~/.cmd0/project
npm run build
```

We welcome contributions! Please:

1. Fork the repository
2. Create a feature branch
3. Make your changes
4. Submit a pull request

See [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/dvrosalesm/cmd0/issues) for ideas.
